title: Quasi-3D Thermal Simulation of Integrated Circuit Systems in Packages
geometry_or_power: explicit package lid/dies/interposer/substrate/PCB layers; examples include 4 W and 8 W dies
reported_boundary_evidence: top package convection; PCB exterior may be ambient Dirichlet or a specified convective coefficient
p1d_use: supports explicit B stack with independently specified exterior heat exchange
limitation: does not prescribe the full P1d h grid

title: Control Principles and On-Chip Circuits for Active Cooling Using Integrated Superlattice-Based Thin-Film Thermoelectric Devices
system: 9 x 9 mm chip and package with heat spreader
geometry_or_power: 9 x 9 mm chip; 23 x 23 mm spreader; full package/FLUENT validation
p1d_use: supports the upper top-h endpoint for enhanced cooling
limitation: active TEC/package study; not a passive-air coefficient

title: Thermal-Mechanical Models for Non-Conforming Surface Contacts
system: silicon chip and aluminum heat sink
geometry_or_power: 15 x 15 x 0.75 mm silicon; 6 x 6 x 0.5 mm heat sink
reported_boundary_evidence: illustrative microelectronics heat-sink boundary h=500 W/(m2 K)
p1d_use: supports the lower top-h endpoint and branch/contact interpretation
limitation: illustrative contact model rather than a full package experiment

title: Experimental and Numerical Study of Natural Convection for High Powered and Wire-Bonded QFN64b Electronic Device
publication: International Communications in Heat and Mass Transfer
reported_boundary_evidence: measured/numerical correlations vary by top/side/bottom/board surface from about 9 to hundreds of W/(m2 K)
p1d_use: supports asymmetric surface coefficients and bottom 20-200 sensitivity
limitation: local QFN underside coefficient includes package/board conduction effects and is not directly transferable

title: Quantification of the natural convective heat transfer for the tilted and wire-bonded QFN32b-PCB electronic assembly
publication: International Communications in Heat and Mass Transfer
reported_boundary_evidence: separate free-convection coefficients for different package and PCB areas
p1d_use: supports use of distinct top and bottom effective Robin families

title: 3-D conjugate heat transfer analysis of PLCC packages mounted in-line on a Printed Circuit Board
publication: International Communications in Heat and Mass Transfer
system: PLCC packages on PCB in forced airflow
geometry_or_power: 27.2 x 27.2 x 3.8 mm packages; varied power spacing and air velocity
reported_boundary_evidence: conjugate experiments show surface transfer varies with forced-air velocity and package position
p1d_use: supports treating top cooling as an effective forced-convection family
limitation: does not report a directly reusable uniform h grid

title: Thermal and migration induced stresses in microelectronic systems
system: Amkor PoP12/PoP14 with bumps FR4 and three dies
geometry_or_power: 100 um dies; total heat loss 0-2 W; local hotspots
reported_boundary_evidence: free surfaces use HTC=12 W/(m2 K) for natural convection plus radiation
p1d_use: anchors weak-convection scale and motivates 1-2 W near-adiabatic comparison
limitation: 1-2 W/m2K P1d controls are deliberately weaker than the paper's 12 W/m2K

title: Modeling Analysis Design and Tests for Electronics Packaging beyond Moore chapter 3
geometry_or_power: 75 um bump/underfill; 100 um interposer; 1 mm BT substrate; 1.6 mm PCB
reported_boundary_evidence: anisotropic equivalent package blocks preserve routing and through-plane heat paths
p1d_use: inherits the already frozen P1c B material stack
limitation: scholarly chapter rather than a journal primary experiment; no h selection claim
